title: Choose and divide
description:
The binomial coefficient C(m,n) is defined as

            m!
C(m,n) = --------
         n!(m-n)!

Given four natural numbers p, q, r, and s, compute the the result of dividing C(p,q) by C(r,s).
input: Input consists of a sequence of lines. Each line contains four non-negative integer numbers giving values for p, q, r, and s, respectively, separated by a single space. All the numbers will be smaller than 10,000 with p>=q and r>=s.
output: For each line of input, print a single line containing a real number with 5 digits of precision in the fraction, giving the number as described above. You may assume the result is not greater than 100,000,000.
source: Contest, Waterloo's local Programming Contests, 2 October, 1999
